handoff.reason: plan_created; Phase 1 (P0 correctness & trust) fully authored and ready; Phases 2-3 deferred (rolling plan)

execution_rules: Rolling plan: execute phase-by-phase; report back after EVERY phase (rolling_plan.report_back_required_after_each_phase=true)

Phases 2-3 are plan_deferred placeholders — author their tasks only after the prior phase is completed + reviewed (ad-hoc plan.json edit until /plan-next-phase ships; flip the bound stub deferred_phase->promoted when authored)
Hard stop at the end of every phase; hard_stops[].approved flips only on operator review
Commit on main by EXPLICIT path (main-only git model; never git add -A)
Do not regress the canonical pipeline: locked index.json/validation schemas and invariant tests must keep passing
hard_constraints: LLM/embedding work is Ollama LOCAL-ONLY: no cloud API keys exist in this environment (operator directive 2026-07-02). RAG tests mock or use ollama; rag-server/provider defaults must not require OpenAI/Anthropic keys
Windows-first path testing on every path-related change
layers.json resolves from the sibling ASSISTANT repo, never a bundled copy
Reconcile STUB-9F63EJ overlap before authoring Phase 2 tasks
Element-count re-baseline after scanner exclude change must be an explicit, reviewed commit

phase2_gate_events.detail: Same constraint-cloud-rag critical as Phase 1 (substring match at semantic-validator.js:333-354). This run it ALSO matches P2-T11's task text, whose sentence is the Ollama-LOCAL-ONLY enforcement itself ('must never require OpenAI/Anthropic keys') — the match cannot distinguish forbidding cloud providers from wiring them. No Phase 2 task introduces a cloud provider; P2-T11 hard-codes the local-only constraint. False positive; proceeding. Warning dep-P2-T4 ('edits undefined') is a validator parsing wart, noted.

plan_adjustments.detail: Legacy-stack deletion swept 4 test files beyond the plan's touches list: __tests__/analyzer.test.ts + graph-helpers.test.ts + phase5-integration.test.ts (test the deleted stack itself) DELETED; orphan-detection.test.ts DELETED (tests legacy GraphBuilder's orphans feature, no canonical equivalent); accuracy-validation.test.ts REDUCED to its scanner-only Accuracy Metrics block (edge accuracy now pinned by pipeline ground-truth tests + the new P1-8 scanner test). AC-07 guard test flipped from 'marked @legacy' to 'file removed' (the removal arm). Repo-root index.ts also re-exported the deleted modules (not just src/index.ts) — both rewired to canonical-graph.

strict pipeline typecheck's import closure pulls in 3 analyzer/generator files outside src/pipeline; 9 errors fixed (typed-conditional array pushes, PackageJson index access, elem.async ?? false) + @types/babel__traverse added as devDependency (types-only). Anthropic default generation model is now MODEL_REGISTRY-sourced (claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, was hardcoded claude-3-sonnet-20240229 in the removed copies) — moot in this Ollama-local-only environment.

Baseline vitest is ALREADY GREEN (1662 pass / 30 skip / 0 fail; review baseline was 1660/2/30). Both review-time failures are load-dependent flakes, reproducible only under concurrent I/O: (a) buildDependencyGraph ENOENT = Windows delete-pending race on the reused .test-project dir (source mkdir already recursive:true); (b) the 'recursive-mode' failure is __tests__/scanner-export.test.ts:170 'should support recursive option' (scans live __dirname twice, count comparison races with concurrent scans) - NOT src/scanner/__tests__/ast-mode.test.ts as the plan cited (citation drift). P1-T10 re-scoped to: harden both flaky tests deterministically (unique temp dirs; fixture-based recursive comparison).

plan_adjustments.reason: Parity instrument mis-targeted at authoring: populate/index.json does NOT consume scanCurrentElements (pipeline has its own tree-sitter ElementExtractor/RelationshipExtractor via orchestrator.ts). The scanner.ts perf fixes affect the scan CLI / file-watcher / context-generator path only. Probe evidence: default scanCurrentElements over repo=63-71s (reproduces review 71.9s), regex double-pass contributes 3358 noise identities (if:/catch:/fetch( pseudo-elements) on tree-sitter-succeeded files.
plan_adjustments.adjustment: P3-T6 gate re-scoped: (a) populate index.json element identity set must be UNCHANGED (2504/268); (b) scanCurrentElements diff may contain ONLY removals on tree-sitter-succeeded files (regex double-scan noise); files where tree-sitter throws must keep their regex elements (true per-file fallback); (c) full suite green is the behavioral guard.

plan_adjustments.reason: Full-suite run exposed that the regex pass was NOT pure noise: route detection (Flask/FastAPI/Express, el.route) and frontend-call metadata are regex-pattern features (extractMetadata/extractFrontendCall callbacks) with no tree-sitter equivalent — 13 route-integration tests failed. Also: the pipeline ElementExtractor emits Class.method qualified names while the scanner contract is bare method names (2 more failures), and the watch-debounce smoke script is timing-sensitive under full-suite CPU load.
plan_adjustments.adjustment: (a) After tree-sitter success the scanner now runs a METADATA-ONLY regex pass (only patterns bearing extractMetadata/extractFrontendCall; matches without extracted metadata are dropped via new processFile metadataOnly flag) — full element regex scan stays skipped, perf win retained. (b) deduplicateElements merges route/frontendCall/calls/imports across colliding elements instead of dropping the loser's knowledge. (c) tree-sitter-file-scan strips method name qualifiers to preserve the scanner's bare-name contract. (d) mjs smoke runner got retry:2 for wall-clock-sensitive scripts.

communication_log.entry: Workorder created via stub promotion (STUB-W45PTN -> WO-REPO-REVIEW-2026-07-REMEDIATION-001); rolling plan with deferred phases bound to STUB-XG7DSB / STUB-01DW28.

phase3_gate_events.detail: rg pass: phase-3 topic surfaces present (stub.json in coderef/working/repo-review-2026-07-remediation; context.md carries 4 P2 anchor rows). Deep inventory already verified by read-only scout pre-authoring; no drift between plan targets and tree.

phase3_gate_events.override_evidence: Critical constraint-violation (Cloud LLM provider referenced) fires on the literal token anthropic inside P3-T7, whose ACTION is deleting src/semantic/llm-enricher.ts — the last Anthropic-referencing module — i.e. the task ENFORCES the ollama-local-only constraint. No P3 task adds a cloud provider or key requirement; provider-factory default stays ollama. 5th recurrence of the substring-match bug (semantic-validator.js:333-354), tracked as STUB-6MM7W2. Warning dependency-violation is a shape artifact: validator reads task.target, this plan uses touches[].

phase3_gate_events.detail: Scan CLI default full-repo scan 71.1s -> 8.2s (~8.7x; review target single-digit seconds HIT). Populate parity: index.json element identity set unchanged outside the phase's own source edits (24 missing / 28 extra, 100% confined to the 5 edited scanner/analyzer files — line shifts + new helper functions; 0 deltas elsewhere). Pipeline wall 21.4s -> 19.6s (pipeline has its own extraction; unaffected as predicted by ADJ-04). Scanner+analyzer suites 195/195 after edits, including the 11 tests the old double-scan behavior was breaking (ast-mode precedence + parallel fixture recall).

Scanner's private TreeSitterScanner.extractTypeScriptElements missed interfaces, constants and type aliases (probe: 5-construct fixture yielded only function/class/method). The regex double-pass was masking the gap. Fixed by routing scanner element extraction through the pipeline ElementExtractor (same tree, one parse) — P1-8 precedent. TreeSitterScanner is now production-orphaned (only its own test remains) — flagged for a future deletion stub, NOT deleted this phase (unplanned scope).
